steps.title: Identify the Contrast Signal
steps.content: The strongest clue is the word But that immediately follows the underlined sentence. This transitional word signals that Arnold is about to present his own position in direct opposition to what The Times just said.

steps.title: Apply the Signal to the Choices
steps.content: Choice D correctly describes the sentence's function: it presents The Times' opinion, which Arnold then rejects. The other choices misidentify the sentence's function — A and B attribute positions to it that it doesn't express, while C falsely treats it as supporting Arnold's argument instead of opposing it. That matches choice D — D is correct.
